heading: Coordination with National Drug Control Program agencies in demand reduction, supply reduction, and State and local affairs
label: 1704 Coordination with National Drug Control Program agencies in demand reduction, supply reduction, and State and local affairs

children.label: A
children.text: drug control; or

children.label: B
children.text: the manner in which amounts made available to that agency for drug control are being used by that agency.

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: The authorities conferred on the Office and the Director by this chapter shall be exercised in a manner consistent with provisions of the National Security Act of 1947 [ et seq.]. The Director of National Intelligence shall prescribe such regulations as may be necessary to protect information provided pursuant to this chapter regarding intelligence sources and methods.50 U.S.C. 3001

children.heading: Duties of Director
children.label: B Duties of Director
children.text: The Director of National Intelligence and the Director of the Central Intelligence Agency shall, to the maximum extent practicable in accordance with subparagraph (A), render full assistance and support to the Office and the Director.

children.heading: Secretaries of the Interior and Agriculture
children.label: A Secretaries of the Interior and Agriculture
children.text: Not later than July 1 of each year, the Secretaries of Agriculture and the Interior shall jointly submit to the Director and the appropriate congressional committees an assessment of the quantity of illegal drug cultivation and manufacturing in the United States on lands owned or under the jurisdiction of the Federal Government for the preceding year.

children.label: i
children.text: the number and type of seizures of drugs by each component of the Department of Homeland Security seizing drugs, as well as statistical information on the geographic areas of such seizures; and

children.label: ii
children.text: the number of air and maritime patrol hours primarily dedicated to drug supply reduction missions undertaken by each component of the Department of Homeland Security.

children.heading: Secretary of Defense
children.label: C Secretary of Defense
children.text: The Secretary of Defense shall, by July 1 of each year, submit to the Director and the appropriate congressional committees information for the preceding year regarding the number of air and maritime patrol hours primarily dedicated to drug supply reduction missions undertaken by each component of the Department of Defense.

children.label: i
children.text: arrests for drug violations;

children.label: ii
children.text: prosecutions for drug violations by United States Attorneys; and

children.label: iii
children.text: seizures of drugs by each component of the Department of Justice seizing drugs, as well as statistical information on the geographic areas of such seizures.

children.heading: Certification of policy changes to Director
children.label: b Certification of policy changes to Director

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: Subject to paragraph (2), the head of a National Drug Control Program agency shall, unless exigent circumstances require otherwise, notify the Director in writing regarding any proposed change in policies relating to the activities of that agency under the National Drug Control Program prior to implementation of such change. The Director shall promptly review such proposed change and certify to the head of that agency in writing whether such change is consistent with the National Drug Control Strategy.

children.label: A
children.text: the head of the National Drug Control Program agency shall notify the Director of the proposed change as soon as practicable; and

children.label: B
children.text: upon such notification, the Director shall review the change and certify to the head of that agency in writing whether the change is consistent with the National Drug Control Strategy.

children.heading: General Services Administration
children.label: c General Services Administration
children.text: The Administrator of General Services shall provide to the Director, on a reimbursable basis, such administrative support services as the Director may request.

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: Not later than February 1 of each year, in accordance with guidance issued by the Director, the head of each National Drug Control Program agency shall submit to the Director a detailed accounting of all funds expended by the agency for National Drug Control Program activities during the previous fiscal year and shall ensure such detailed accounting is authenticated for the previous fiscal year by the Inspector General for such agency prior to the submission to the Director as frequently as determined by the Inspector General but not less frequently than every 3 years.

children.heading: Submission to Congress
children.label: 2 Submission to Congress
children.text: The Director shall submit to Congress not later than April 1 of each year the information submitted to the Director under paragraph (1).

children.heading: Drug court training and technical assistance program
children.label: e Drug court training and technical assistance program

children.heading: Grants authorized
children.label: 1 Grants authorized
children.text: The Director may make a grant to a nonprofit organization for the purpose of providing training and technical assistance to drug courts.

children.heading: Authorization of appropriations
children.label: 2 Authorization of appropriations
children.text: There is authorized to be appropriated to carry out this subsection $2,000,000 for each of fiscal years 2018 through 2023.

children.heading: Tracking system for federally funded grant programs
children.label: f Tracking system for federally funded grant programs
i
children.text: all drug control grants and pertinent identifying information for each grant; and

children.label: ii
children.text: any available performance metrics, evaluations, or other information indicating the effectiveness of such programs;

children.label: B
children.text: facilitate efforts to identify duplication, overlap, or gaps in funding to provide increased accountability of Federally-funded grants for substance use disorder treatment, prevention, and enforcement; and

children.label: C
children.text: identify barriers that may impede applicants in the grant application process.

children.heading: National Drug Control Program Agencies
children.label: 2 National Drug Control Program Agencies
children.text: The head of each National Drug Control Program agency shall provide to the Director a complete list of all drug control program grant programs and any other relevant information for inclusion in the system developed under paragraph (1) and annually update such list.

children.heading: Updating existing systems
children.label: 3 Updating existing systems
children.text: The Director may meet the requirements of this subsection by utilizing, updating, or improving existing Federal information systems to ensure they meet the requirements of this subsection.

children.heading: Report
children.label: 4 Report
children.text: Not later than 3 years after , the Comptroller General of the United States shall submit to Congress a report examining implementation of this subsection.October 24, 2018
